title: Living Your Life for Today

scripture.text: This is the day that the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it.

body: I know so many people today who live their lives in the past. They dwell on how much better things used to be, how bad they are today, and the best way to turn back the clock and get things back to how they were before it all went so wrong!

And you know, I'm grateful for the past. I'm grateful for what God has done in the Old Testament, the New Testament, and leading up to today throughout the history of the Church. I also love that you and I can look back and see how He has remained the same yesterday and today, and by that, we can know He'll be faithful tomorrow!

But today is God's day. God is no less responsible for creating this day than He is for the first day of creation. And He has called you and me on this day to make a difference for His Kingdom in our times.

Maybe today you find yourself looking backwards on the journey of life. Maybe you've thought, 'I just wish God would work today like He used to work in the past.' But let me tell you that God is as alive and active today as He's ever been in history! So trust in Him to work in and through you today and just watch what He will do!
keyTruth: Live your life not by looking backward, but by observing how God is working in and through you today!

title: The Peace That Passes Understanding

scripture.text: And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.

body: There is a peace available to every believer that the world simply cannot manufacture. You can search for it in a career, in a relationship, in wealth or in recognition — and you will never find it. That kind of peace has a shelf life. It expires.

But the peace that Paul writes about in Philippians is different. It is a supernatural calm that settles over your heart even in the middle of a storm. It doesn't mean the storm is gone. It means you know the One who holds the storm in His hands.

Today, whatever you are facing — a difficult diagnosis, a broken relationship, a financial crisis — God is inviting you to bring it to Him in prayer. Not as a last resort, but as your first instinct. When you lay your burdens at His feet, He stands guard over your heart and mind like a sentinel who never sleeps.
You don't have to understand everything to have peace. You just have to trust the One who does.
keyTruth: You don't need to understand your circumstances — you need to trust the God who is in control of every one of them!

title: Strength for the Weary

scripture.text: He gives power to the faint, and to him who has no might He increases strength.

body: Have you ever felt like you simply had nothing left? Like the tank was empty and there was still a hundred miles to go? That feeling is not a sign of failure. It is a very human experience — and God knew we would face it.

Isaiah 40 was written to a people in exile. They were exhausted, discouraged, and far from home. And in the middle of their weariness, God speaks one of the most beautiful promises in all of Scripture: He gives strength to the weak.

Notice that God doesn't say, 'Get yourself together and then come to Me.' He meets you in your weakness. In fact, Paul discovered in 2 Corinthians that God's power is actually made perfect in our weakness. That means your greatest moments of impact may come out of your greatest moments of depletion — because that's when His strength shines through without any of ours getting in the way.
If you are weary today, do not white-knuckle your way through it. Open your hands. Ask God to fill you with the strength that only He can provide. He is faithful — and He will come through.
keyTruth: When you are at your weakest, you are in the perfect position to experience God's greatest strength!
